243	\subsection{The Albanese witness bundle}
244	
245	Before stating the existence theorem, we package its conclusion into a single structure --- the \emph{Albanese witness} of $C$. This bundling reverses the quantifier order of the classical statement (see Remark~\ref{rem:JacobianWitness_quantifier_order}) and is the data structure consumed by the \texttt{Jacobian} family of declarations in this file and by the \texttt{AbelJacobi} interface of Chapter~\ref{chap:AbelJacobi}.
246	
247	\begin{definition}
248	
249	\leanok
250	[Albanese witness for a curve]
251	  \label{def:JacobianWitness}
252	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.JacobianWitness}
253	  \uses{def:IsAlbanese, def:genus}
254	  An \emph{Albanese witness} for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C$ over $k$ is a bundle of the following seven fields:
255	  \begin{itemize}
256	    \item $J$: an object of the category of $k$-schemes over $\Spec k$ --- the candidate Albanese object.
257	    \item $\mathtt{grpObj}$: a group-object structure on $J$ in the category of $k$-schemes over $\Spec k$.
258	    \item $\mathtt{proper}$: a proof that the structure morphism $J \to \Spec k$ is proper.
259	    \item $\mathtt{smooth}$: a proof that the structure morphism $J \to \Spec k$ is smooth.
260	    \item $\mathtt{geomIrred}$: a proof that the structure morphism $J \to \Spec k$ is geometrically irreducible.
261	    \item $\mathtt{smoothGenus}$: a proof that the structure morphism $J \to \Spec k$ is smooth of relative dimension $g(C)$. This refines $\mathtt{smooth}$ at the specific relative dimension $\genus\, C$ (see Remark~\ref{rem:JacobianWitness_smooth_redundancy}).
262	    \item $\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}$: a proof that, for every $k$-rational marked point $P \in C(k)$, the scheme $J$ together with its group-object, proper, smooth, and geometrically-irreducible structures satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve $(C, P)$ in the sense of Definition~\ref{def:IsAlbanese}.
263	  \end{itemize}
264	  Formally, an Albanese witness is a Lean-level record (a structure) whose name is \texttt{JacobianWitness}.
265	\end{definition}
266	
267	\begin{remark}
268	  \label{rem:JacobianWitness_quantifier_order}
269	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness}
270	  The classical phrasing of the existence statement of an Albanese variety is
271	  \[
272	    \forall\, P \in C(k),\ \exists\, J,\ J \text{ is an Albanese object for } (C, P).
273	  \]
274	  The witness bundle reverses these quantifiers: a single $J$ is chosen once (intrinsic to $C$), and the marked-point-dependent data is recorded as the family-valued field
275	  \[
276	    \mathtt{isAlbaneseFor} \colon \forall\, P \in C(k),\ \mathrm{IsAlbanese}\,C\,P\,J.
277	  \]
278	  Mathematically this reversal is justified because the underlying scheme of any Albanese object is intrinsic to $C$: a different choice of $P \in C(k)$ alters the universal morphism $\iota_P \colon C \to J$ by a translation of $J$, which is an automorphism of $J$, and so leaves $J$ unchanged as a scheme. The reversed bundling is what makes the per-$P$ projection in Chapter~\ref{chap:AbelJacobi} clean: $\mathtt{Jacobian.ofCurve}$, $\mathtt{Jacobian.comp\_ofCurve}$, and $\mathtt{Jacobian.exists\_unique\_ofCurve\_comp}$ each take a marked point $P$ as input and project the corresponding component of $\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}\,P$ via the extraction trio (Definition~\ref{def:IsAlbanese_ofCurve}, Lemmas~\ref{lem:IsAlbanese_comp_ofCurve}, \ref{lem:IsAlbanese_exists_unique_ofCurve_comp}).
279	\end{remark}
280	
281	\begin{remark}
282	  \label{rem:JacobianWitness_smooth_redundancy}
283	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness}
284	  The fields $\mathtt{smooth}$ and $\mathtt{smoothGenus}$ of an Albanese witness are mathematically redundant: the relative-dimension version implies the unqualified version via Mathlib's \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension.smooth}. Keeping both fields is a Lean-level convenience: $\mathtt{smooth}$ is the form required by the typeclass binder of \texttt{IsAlbanese} (and by the protected $\mathtt{Jacobian.instGrpObj}$ projection), whereas $\mathtt{smoothGenus}$ is the form required by the protected $\mathtt{Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_genus}$ projection. The redundancy carries no mathematical cost (the two fields agree) and no soundness cost (deriving one from the other would only re-run the same Mathlib instance synthesis at the projection sites).
285	\end{remark}
286	
287	\begin{theorem}\leanok
288	[Existence of an Albanese variety uniformly over the marked point]
289	  \label{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}
290	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.nonempty_jacobianWitness}
291	  % Cycle-fix (iter-149 plan-agent): drop the spurious \uses{def:Jacobian}
292	  % from this statement-block. Mathematically `def:Jacobian` is the entity
293	  % that depends on this existence theorem (def:Jacobian's \uses{...} lists
294	  % thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness), not the other way around. Listing
295	  % def:Jacobian here as a hypothesis of the existence theorem introduced
296	  % a 2-cycle in plastex's depgraph (def:Jacobian ↔ thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness)
297	  % that crashes `leanblueprint web` with RecursionError in depgraph.ancestors.
298	  \uses{def:IsAlbanese, def:genus, def:JacobianWitness}
299	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman, The Picard scheme], Exercise ex:jac (generalized Jacobians of a family of curves form a smooth quasi-projective family of relative dimension $p_a$, projective iff $X/S$ smooth); arXiv:math/0504020, p.~50  (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex)
300	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Assume $X/S$ is projective and flat, its geometric fibers
301	  % are integral curves of arithmetic genus $p_a$, and $S$ is Noetherian.
302	  % Show the ``generalized Jacobians'' $\IPicz_{X_s/k_s}$ form a smooth
303	  % quasi-projective family of relative dimension $p_a$.  And show this
304	  % family is projective if and only if $X/S$ is smooth."
305	  \textit{Source: Kleiman, The Picard Scheme, Exercise (ex:jac).}
306	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field $k$. There exists a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme $J$ over $k$ of relative dimension $g(C)$ such that, for every $k$-rational point $P \in C(k)$, the scheme $J$ is an Albanese object for the pointed curve $(C, P)$ in the sense of Definition~\ref{def:IsAlbanese}. Equivalently, the type of Albanese witnesses for $C$ (Definition~\ref{def:JacobianWitness}) is non-empty.
307	\end{theorem}
308	
309	\begin{proof}
310	  \uses{def:IsAlbanese, def:genus, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen, def:genusZeroWitness, thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}
311	  The proof is split into three classical constructions plus the genus-$0$ sub-case absorbed into the same witness (the latter packaged as the explicit construction \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}, which routes through the characteristic-free keystone \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} --- rigidity proved over the algebraic closure $\bar k = \mathrm{AlgebraicClosure}\,k$ --- and then \emph{descends} the resulting morphism equality from $\bar k$ to the base field $k$ along the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$). Each route produces the same intrinsic scheme $J$; only the universal morphism $\iota_P \colon C \to J$ varies with the marked point $P$. We outline each route and name the Mathlib infrastructure each one requires.
312	
313	  \paragraph{(0) Reduction to a $P$-independent witness.}
314	  The witness $J$ depends only on $C$, not on the marked point $P$. Indeed, both classical constructions below produce $J$ as an intrinsic object of $C$ (the identity component of $\Pic_{C/k}$, or the Stein-factorisation target of $\Sym^g(C) \to \Pic^g_{C/k}$ translated by a fixed $P_0$); a different choice of $P$ in $C(k)$ alters $\iota_P$ by a translation of $J$, which is an automorphism, and so does not change $J$ as a scheme. The Lean encoding reflects this by bundling $J$ as a single field and the universal-property data as $\forall P,\, \mathrm{IsAlbanese}\, C\, P\, J$. We now turn to producing $J$.
315	
316	  \paragraph{Route A --- Picard scheme.}
317	  This route follows Hartshorne~III.4 and FGA Explained, Chapter~9.
318	
319	  \begin{itemize}
320	    \item[\textbf{(A.1)}] \emph{The relative Picard functor.} For a $k$-scheme $T$, write $C_T := C \times_k T$ and let $\pi \colon C_T \to T$ be the projection. The relative Picard functor is
321	    \[
322	      \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k} \colon (\Sch/k)^{\mathrm{op}} \to \Sets, \qquad
323	      T \longmapsto \Pic(C_T) / \pi^*\Pic(T).
324	    \]
325	    A $T$-point is a line bundle on $C_T$ modulo a line bundle pulled back from $T$. The set-valued formulation suffices for representability; the canonical abelian-group structure on $\Pic(C_T)$ descends to the quotient by $\pi^*\Pic(T)$ and gives $\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ a natural group-functor refinement.
326	
327	    \item[\textbf{(A.2)}] \emph{Representability.} For $C$ smooth proper geometrically connected over $k$ with a $k$-rational point, $\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ (after \'etale sheafification, if $k$ is not algebraically closed) is representable by a $k$-group scheme $\Pic_{C/k}$, locally of finite type. This is FGA's main representability theorem (Grothendieck, FGA~232); the smooth-proper-geometrically-connected hypothesis ensures that the cohomology-and-base-change machinery applies and that $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) = k$, which is needed for the quotient by $\pi^*\Pic(T)$ to be \'etale-locally injective.
328	
329	    \item[\textbf{(A.3)}] \emph{The identity component.} Let $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ be the identity component of $\Pic_{C/k}$. As the identity component of a $k$-group scheme locally of finite type, $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ is geometrically irreducible; for $C$ smooth proper of genus $g$, it is moreover a smooth proper $k$-group scheme of dimension $g$. Smoothness comes from the deformation-theoretic identification of its tangent space at the identity with $H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$ and homogeneity of the group action; properness from the fact that the natural map $\Pic^0_{C/k} \to \Pic_{C/k}$ is an open and closed immersion onto a proper component (a consequence of the cohomological criterion: $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ is the kernel of the degree map $\Pic_{C/k} \to \underline{\mathbb Z}_k$, which is locally constant). Take $J := \Pic^0_{C/k}$.
330	
331	    \item[\textbf{(A.4)}] \emph{The Abel--Jacobi universal morphism.} For each $P \in C(k)$, the Abel--Jacobi morphism
332	    \[
333	      \iota_P \colon C \to \Pic^0_{C/k}, \qquad
334	      Q \longmapsto [\mathcal O_C(Q - P)]
335	    \]
336	    sends $P$ to the identity of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$. It is universal among morphisms $f \colon C \to A$ to smooth proper geometrically irreducible $k$-group schemes $A$ with $f(P) = \eta_A$: this is the classical Albanese property of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, proved by reducing to the case $k = \overline k$ (Galois descent), where it is a standard consequence of the autoduality of the Jacobian and the fact that line bundles on $C \times A$ trivialised along $\{P\} \times A$ correspond bijectively to morphisms $C \to A^{\vee} = A$ sending $P$ to $\eta_A$.
337	  \end{itemize}
338	
339	  \emph{Mathlib status for Route A.} Each sub-step is missing.
340	  \begin{itemize}
341	    \item Sub-step A.1 requires a working theory of relative line bundles on a product $C \times_k T$ and the descent of the abelian-group structure on $\Pic$ along $\pi^*$. The pieces are present individually but not assembled.
342	    \item Sub-step A.2 requires FGA representability for $\Pic_{C/k}$, which in turn requires Hilbert / Quot scheme infrastructure not in Mathlib (\texttt{HilbertScheme}, \texttt{QuotScheme}, and the cohomology-and-base-change for proper morphisms with finitely presented kernels). The construction engine for this Quot/Hilbert layer is Nitsure, ``Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes'' (FGA Explained; arXiv:math/0504590), \S\,4 (generic flatness and the flattening stratification) and \S\,5 (the construction of $\mathrm{Quot}$, hence $\mathrm{Hilb}$, as a scheme); see \texttt{references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.md}.
343	    \item Sub-step A.3 requires connectedness machinery for group schemes locally of finite type, the identity-component construction $G^0 \subseteq G$, and the degree map $\Pic_{C/k} \to \underline{\mathbb Z}_k$.
344	    \item Sub-step A.4 requires the universal property of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, which is the classical Albanese functoriality and is not in Mathlib in this form.
345	  \end{itemize}
346	
347	  \paragraph{Route B --- Symmetric powers and Stein factorisation (historical alternative; not pursued).}
348	  \textbf{Iter-144 disposition.} Route B is a historical alternative \emph{not pursued} by the project. It is preserved here for scholarly context only. Route A --- Picard scheme via FGA --- is the committed M3 route per STRATEGY.md \S~M3 iter-144 disposition. The decomposition, gating Mathlib pieces, and midpoint LOC figure ($\sim 9000$ LOC) recorded below are the iter-123 audit values (\texttt{analogies/m3-route-audit.md}); the chapter does not re-cost Route B under current Mathlib snapshots.
349	
350	  This route follows Milne's \emph{Abelian Varieties} (Chapter~III) and avoids FGA representability at the cost of requiring quotients of schemes by finite group actions.
351	
352	  \begin{itemize}
353	    \item[\textbf{(B.1)}] \emph{The $g$-fold symmetric power.} Form the product $C^g$ and let $S_g$ act by permutation of the factors. The categorical quotient $C^{(g)} := C^g / S_g$ exists as a $k$-scheme, smooth and proper over $k$ of dimension $g$. Smoothness follows because the action of $S_g$ on the smooth $k$-scheme $C^g$ is free outside the diagonal locus and the local-ring computation at the diagonal points produces a smooth ring (this is the symmetric-product computation of Fogarty / Mumford). Properness descends from properness of $C^g$ via the geometric-quotient property.
354	
355	    \item[\textbf{(B.2)}] \emph{The Abel--Jacobi morphism on symmetric powers.} Fix a $k$-rational point $P_0 \in C(k)$. The map
356	    \[
357	      \sigma \colon C^{(g)} \longrightarrow \Pic^g_{C/k}, \qquad
358	      \sum_{i=1}^g [Q_i] \longmapsto \mathcal O_C\bigl(\textstyle\sum_{i=1}^g Q_i\bigr)
359	    \]
360	    is well-defined (the right-hand side is symmetric in the $Q_i$ and depends only on the unordered tuple) and is surjective. By Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch, $\sigma$ is birational onto its image: a generic divisor of degree $g$ on $C$ has $h^0(\mathcal O_C(D)) = 1$, so the fibre $\sigma^{-1}([\mathcal O_C(D)])$ is a point at generic divisors; the special fibres are projective spaces $\mathbb P(h^0 - 1)$ over the locus where $h^0 > 1$.
361	
362	    \item[\textbf{(B.3)}] \emph{The Stein factorisation.} Because $\sigma$ is a proper surjection with geometrically connected fibres (each fibre $\mathbb P^{h^0-1}_k$ is geometrically irreducible), its Stein factorisation
363	    \[
364	      C^{(g)} \xrightarrow{\;\sigma_*\;} \Spec_{\Pic^g_{C/k}}\bigl(\sigma_* \mathcal O_{C^{(g)}}\bigr) \longrightarrow \Pic^g_{C/k}
365	    \]
366	    presents the second arrow as a finite morphism whose source is the intermediate scheme. Since the fibres of $\sigma$ are connected, this intermediate scheme equals $\Pic^g_{C/k}$ scheme-theoretically (the Stein factorisation is trivial on the second arrow when fibres are connected, by Stein's classical theorem). The middle scheme is therefore $\Pic^g_{C/k}$ itself, which is smooth proper geometrically irreducible of dimension $g$. Translating by $-g P_0$ yields $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, and we take $J := \Pic^0_{C/k}$ as in Route A. The universal property is then established as in Sub-step A.4.
367	  \end{itemize}
368	
369	  \emph{Mathlib status for Route B.} Each sub-step is blocked by a different missing piece.
370	  \begin{itemize}
371	    \item Sub-step B.1 requires symmetric powers of schemes (the construction $\Sym^g$ for a $k$-scheme of finite type) and finite-group-scheme quotients of schemes (the quotient $C^g / S_g$). Mathlib has finite-group quotients of types and of various algebraic structures, but not yet a usable scheme-level quotient by a finite group action with the smooth-target property.
372	    \item Sub-step B.2 requires Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch, including the relative version for families of curves --- the cohomology-and-base-change machinery for proper morphisms.
373	    \item Sub-step B.3 requires the Stein factorisation theorem for proper morphisms of locally Noetherian schemes ($f \colon X \to Y$ proper $\implies$ $f_* \mathcal O_X$ is a coherent $\mathcal O_Y$-algebra). Mathlib has partial coherence-theoretic infrastructure but does not yet have the cohomology of $\mathcal O$-modules of finite type along proper morphisms in the form needed.
374	  \end{itemize}
375	
376	  \paragraph{Genus-$0$ sub-case (absorbed into the same witness).}
377	  When $g(C) = 0$ the witness's underlying scheme is $J = \Spec k$, and the universal property reduces to a rigidity statement.
378	
379	  \begin{itemize}
380	    \item[\textbf{(C.1)}] \emph{Brauer--Severi identification.} A smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C$ over $k$ with $g(C) = 0$ and a $k$-rational point $P \in C(k)$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_k$. This is the Brauer--Severi triviality criterion: a Brauer--Severi variety of dimension $1$ is $\mathbb P^1$ over its base if and only if it has a rational point. The genus-$0$ identification on the curve side uses $h^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2$ (Riemann--Roch) to produce a degree-$1$ morphism $C \to \mathbb P^1_k$, which is then an isomorphism by degree-and-dimension.
381	
382	    \item[\textbf{(C.2)}] \emph{Rigidity for morphisms from $\mathbb P^1$ to an abelian variety.} We state the rigidity result over the algebraic closure $\bar k$, since the protected signature of Theorem~\ref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} does not assume $C(k) \neq \emptyset$ and the genus-$0$ identification $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ is only available after passing to $\bar k$ (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$ are the standard counterexamples to a $k$-side identification without a rational point). The conclusion over $k$ --- equality of the given morphism with the constant morphism as $k$-schemes --- then follows by \emph{descent}: a morphism equality that holds after base change to $\bar k$ descends to $k$ along the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (Sub-step~C.2.f).
383	
384	    \begin{itemize}
385	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.a)}] \emph{Statement (over $\bar k$).} Let $A$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $\bar k$, with identity element $\eta_A \in A(\bar k)$. Let
386	      \[
387	        f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \longrightarrow A
388	      \]
389	      be a morphism of $\bar k$-schemes such that $f(p) = \eta_A$ for some closed point $p \in \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}(\bar k)$. Then $f$ factors as the composite $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k \xrightarrow{\,\eta_A\,} A$; equivalently, $f$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$.
390	
391	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.b)}] \emph{Reduction to the project's rigidity lemma.} Let $c \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ denote the constant morphism at $\eta_A$ --- the unique $\bar k$-morphism factoring through the closed-point section $\eta_A \colon \Spec \bar k \hookrightarrow A$. The conclusion $f = c$ follows from Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} applied to $g_1 := f$, $g_2 := c$, $X := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$, $Y := A$, once we produce a non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ on which $U.\iota \circ f = U.\iota \circ c$ as scheme morphisms. (After the iter-125 refactor, the cited theorem is stated at the scheme level: source reduced and geometrically irreducible, target separated; the source-side group-object decoration that the earlier formulation carried has been dropped. $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ satisfies the refactored hypotheses trivially --- it is smooth, hence reduced; it is geometrically irreducible over $\bar k$; and $A$ is proper hence separated over $\bar k$.) The reduction therefore amounts to: produce a non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ on which $f$ and $c$ agree as scheme morphisms; we will in fact take $U = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.
392	
393	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.c)}] \emph{Image dimension argument.} The morphism $f$ goes from a proper geometrically integral $\bar k$-scheme of dimension $1$ to a smooth proper $\bar k$-scheme of dimension $g := \dim_{\bar k} A$. The set-theoretic image $f(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) \subseteq A$ is an irreducible closed subset of $A$ (the continuous image of an irreducible space under a proper map is irreducible and closed) of dimension at most $1$. If $g = 0$ then $A = \Spec \bar k$ and the conclusion is vacuous: the unique $\bar k$-morphism to $\Spec \bar k$ is the constant morphism. Henceforth assume $g \geq 1$, in which case the image is either a single point (the desired conclusion) or a one-dimensional irreducible proper closed subscheme of $A$ --- an irreducible rational curve in $A$.
394	
395	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.d)}] \emph{Key classical input: morphisms from $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ to an abelian variety are constant.} The remaining case --- image of dimension exactly $1$ --- is ruled out by the classical fact that, for an abelian variety $A$ of positive dimension over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$, every morphism $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant. The project ranks three proof routes for this fact; route~(c) is \textbf{committed}, with (b) and (a) as fallbacks.
396	      \begin{itemize}
397	        \item[\textbf{(c) [committed; characteristic-free]}] \emph{Milne \S I.3 rigidity chain (cube-free).} Over $\bar k$ a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}), and every morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ to an abelian variety is constant (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); a fortiori the morphism $f$ is constant. The constancy of $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is \emph{not} supplied by the theorem of the cube. The base case is the $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ group-law incompatibility (Milne Proposition~3.9, \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}): the additive-defect map $\psi(x,y) = f(x+y) - f(x) - f(y)$ on $\mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a$ is extended to the complete surface $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$ (rational-map extension \cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}, Milne Thm~3.2/3.4) and then collapsed by the \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford Form~I --- an elementary closed-map argument), forcing $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ to be an additive homomorphism; the same argument on $\mathbb G_m$ produces a multiplicative relation incompatible with it unless $f$ is constant. The additivity Corollary~1.5 (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) packages the same collapse over a product. Combining with the genus-$0$ classification yields the headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. This chain uses \emph{neither} the theorem of the cube, \emph{nor} $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre duality, \emph{nor} the Picard representability engine, and is valid in arbitrary characteristic; it is developed in \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}. It is the committed route for the genus-$0$ keystone; the over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which still carries $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$) is the fallback-(a) artifact, not consumed here.
398	
399	        \item[\textbf{(b) [fallback]}] \emph{Via the dual abelian variety.} Pullback of line bundles along $f$ induces a homomorphism of group schemes $f^{\vee} \colon A^{\vee} \to \Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k})$, where $A^{\vee}$ is the dual abelian variety (the moduli space of degree-zero line bundles on $A$). The Picard group of $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ has trivial degree-zero component, $\Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) = 0$, because every line bundle on $\mathbb P^1$ of degree $0$ is isomorphic to $\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}$ (Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, II.6 and II.7). Hence $f^{\vee} = 0$. By autoduality of abelian varieties ($A^{\vee\vee} = A$ canonically, double duality of the Poincar\'e line bundle), the zero of $f^{\vee}$ implies that $f$ itself is constant. This route is free once Route~A's $\Pic^0$ engine exists, but it \emph{couples} the genus-$0$ rigidity to the Route~A representability monster, which the committed route~(c) deliberately avoids.
400	
401	        \item[\textbf{(a) [fallback; off the critical path; carries a characteristic-$p$ gap]}] \emph{Via the cotangent bundle (the differential $df = 0$ route).} The cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{A/\bar k}$ of an abelian variety is a trivial $\mathcal O_A$-module: the tangent bundle of a group scheme over a field is generated globally by translates of the Lie algebra at the identity, hence is trivial, hence so is its dual. Pulling back along $f$ yields a morphism of locally free sheaves $f^{*} \Omega_{A/\bar k} \to \Omega_{\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}/\bar k}$, whose global sections form a $\bar k$-linear map
402	        \[
403	          H^0\bigl(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k},\, f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k}\bigr) \longrightarrow H^0\bigl(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k},\, \Omega_{\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}/\bar k}\bigr) = H^0\bigl(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k},\, \mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(-2)\bigr) = 0.
404	        \]
405	        Triviality of $\Omega_{A/\bar k}$ means the left-hand side is a non-zero $\bar k$-vector space (of dimension $g$); the only way the map can land in zero is for it to be the zero map at every fibre, i.e.\ for the differential $df$ to vanish identically on $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$. Smoothness of $A$ over $\bar k$ together with the vanishing of $df$ forces $f$ to be a constant morphism on the geometric points and hence (by reduced-source / separated-target, see C.2.e) as a scheme morphism. This is the \emph{differential} route: it requires the Serre-duality-flavoured vanishing $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = 0$ together with the cotangent-triviality of $\Omega_A$, is retained in the tree via the closed chart-algebra envelope (which supplies only the converse implication $df = 0 \Rightarrow f$ constant), and carries a characteristic-$p$ gap. It is \emph{not} the committed route.
406	      \end{itemize}
407	      Each route yields: $f$ is constant. The pointed condition $f(p) = \eta_A$ then identifies the constant value, so $f = c$ on underlying topological points.
408	
409	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.e)}] \emph{Promoting set-level equality to scheme equality.} The set-level equality $f = c$ on underlying topological points (established in C.2.d) promotes to scheme-morphism equality on any non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ via the standard reduced-source / separated-target argument: $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ is reduced (smooth implies regular, regular implies reduced) and $A$ is separated over $\bar k$ (proper implies separated), so a $\bar k$-morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is determined by its values on the underlying topological space. In particular, $f|_U = c|_U$ as scheme morphisms for the open $U := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ itself, and the reduction of C.2.b gives $f = c$ as scheme morphisms.
410	
411	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.f)}] \emph{Descent of morphism equality from $\bar k$ to $k$.} The rigidity conclusion of Sub-step~C.2.d is proved over the algebraic closure $\bar k = \mathrm{AlgebraicClosure}\,k$, where the characteristic-free keystone \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (\cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}) carries the hypothesis $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and, being characteristic-free, no $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$. To obtain the corresponding equality of morphisms over the base field $k$, we descend along the base change $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$.
412	
413	      Concretely, let $f, c \colon C \to A$ be two $k$-morphisms (in the application $f$ is the given map and $c$ the constant map at $\eta_A$). Base-change along $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ produces $f_{\bar k}, c_{\bar k} \colon C_{\bar k} \to A_{\bar k}$ together with the projections $p \colon C_{\bar k} = C \times_k \bar k \to C$ and $q \colon A_{\bar k} \to A$, fitting the base-change squares $q \circ f_{\bar k} = f \circ p$ and $q \circ c_{\bar k} = c \circ p$. Suppose $f_{\bar k} = c_{\bar k}$ (this is what \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} delivers over $\bar k$). Then $f \circ p = q \circ f_{\bar k} = q \circ c_{\bar k} = c \circ p$, so $f$ and $c$ agree after pre-composition with $p$.
414	
415	      It remains to right-cancel $p$. The morphism $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ is faithfully flat (a field extension is a faithfully flat ring map) and surjective; both properties are stable under base change, so the projection $p \colon C_{\bar k} \to C$ is faithfully flat and surjective. By \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}, $p$ is therefore an epimorphism of schemes, and right-cancelling it in $f \circ p = c \circ p$ gives $f = c$ over $k$. \emph{This closing epi-cancellation is the only genuinely $\sim 2$-line part.} The descent as a whole is \textbf{not} a $\sim 2$-line step but a multi-iteration base-change sub-build: neither this project nor Mathlib provides a base-change functor $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k) \to \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$, so even producing $f_{\bar k}, c_{\bar k}$ and the base-change squares $q \circ f_{\bar k} = f \circ p$ above first requires (a) that functor; (b) transfer of every relevant instance along it ($\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,1$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$, $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$, $\mathtt{GrpObj}$); (c) stability of $\genus = 0$ under the base change $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ for a geometrically irreducible curve; and (d) the base-change-square commutativity identities. All four must be in place before the closing epi-cancellation applies; only that final cancellation is short, while the surrounding base-change infrastructure is the bulk of the cost.
416	
417	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.g)}] \emph{Mathlib gap statement.} The keystone fact of Sub-step~C.2.d --- that every morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$ to a positive-dimensional smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $\bar k$ is constant --- is \emph{not} present in current Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) in any form usable here. The project records the committed, characteristic-free form as the named declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (carrying $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and \emph{no} $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$ hypothesis); the older over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, whose base field still carries both $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$, is retained only as the fallback-(a) artifact. The \textbf{committed} proof route is route~(c) of C.2.d: the Milne \S I.3 rigidity chain (cube-free) (\cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}, headline \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} feeding \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}), which is characteristic-free and uses neither the theorem of the cube, nor Serre duality, nor the Picard engine. The differential decomposition into the shared cotangent-vanishing pile of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} pieces (i)--(iii) --- (i) cotangent triviality of a group scheme over $\bar k$ via the functorial shear iso; (ii) scheme-level $\mathrm d f = 0 \Rightarrow$ $f$ factors through $\Spec \bar k$ via the \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass plus the iter-125-refactored \texttt{Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}; (iii) characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$ --- is the \textbf{fallback} route~(a), off the critical path but retained in the tree (each piece an upstream Mathlib gap-fill in its own right; see \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}). When $g = \dim A = 0$ the statement is vacuous and the declaration's conclusion is automatic. The reduction step C.2.b reuses the project's already-formalised Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (after the iter-125 refactor, this is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}, stated at the scheme level: source reduced and geometrically irreducible, target separated); the promotion step C.2.e uses standard reduced-source / separated-target lemmas, partially in Mathlib. The descent step C.2.f --- from $\bar k$ back to the base field $k$ --- has a genuinely short \emph{final} move (the epi-cancellation via \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}, already in Mathlib in \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Flat}), but the descent as a whole is \emph{not} a $\sim 2$-line step: it is a multi-iteration base-change sub-build, because neither this project nor Mathlib provides a base-change functor $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k) \to \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$, and the instance transfer, genus-stability, and base-change-square identities (C.2.f items (a)--(d)) must all be built before the closing epi-cancellation applies.
418	    \end{itemize}
419	
420	    \item[\textbf{(C.3)}] \emph{The trivial witness.} Take $J := \Spec k$, the terminal $k$-scheme. The group-object structure on $\Spec k$ is trivial; it is smooth proper geometrically irreducible of relative dimension $0 = g(C)$. The unique morphism $\iota_P \colon C \to \Spec k$ sends $P$ to the (unique) point. By Sub-step C.2 every $f \colon C \to A$ with $f(P) = \eta_A$ is constant at $\eta_A$, hence factors uniquely through the unique morphism $g \colon \Spec k \to A$ sending the point to $\eta_A$; uniqueness of $g$ follows because $\Spec k \to A$ is determined by its value on the unique $k$-rational point of $\Spec k$.
421	  \end{itemize}
422	
423	  \emph{Mathlib status for the genus-$0$ sub-case.}
424	  \begin{itemize}
425	    \item Sub-step C.1 requires the genus-$0$ identification on the curve side, which in turn requires Serre duality / the basic Riemann--Roch infrastructure that this project has explicitly trimmed (see the project's Phase~C exit policy). Mathlib has partial $\mathbb P^1$-theory but the curve-side genus-$0$ identification is missing.
426	    \item Sub-step C.2 reduces, via the project's already-formalised rigidity lemma (Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} of Chapter~\ref{chap:Rigidity}), to the classical fact that proper rational curves on a positive-dimensional smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$ are constant (the keystone of Sub-step~C.2.d, packaged characteristic-free as \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}; the over-$\bar k$ \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} is the retained fallback-(a) artifact). This classical fact is not in Mathlib in the form needed here; see Sub-step~C.2.g for the explicit gap statement and the committed route~(c) chain. The descent of morphism equality from $\bar k$ back to the base field $k$ (Sub-step~C.2.f) has a short \emph{final} epi-cancellation (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}) but is, as a whole, a multi-iteration base-change sub-build rather than a $\sim 2$-line step (see the re-costing at C.2.f).
427	    \item Sub-step C.3 is unconditional once C.1 and C.2 are in place.
428	  \end{itemize}

430	  \paragraph{Mathlib infrastructure summary.}
431	  Per the iter-144 M3 disposition (STRATEGY.md \S~M3), the project commits to Route A for the positive-genus arm and treats Route B as a historical alternative not pursued. The Mathlib build-outs gating the two surviving arms are therefore:
432	  \begin{itemize}
433	    \item[\textbf{($\alpha$)}] \emph{Hilbert / Quot scheme infrastructure plus FGA representability (COMMITTED M3 infrastructure --- iter-144 disposition).} This is the committed Mathlib build-out that unlocks Route A, which in turn discharges the positive-genus arm of the witness existence (\cref{def:positiveGenusWitness}). It is \textbf{mandatory and unconditional for the positive-genus witness \emph{object}}: that object must be a genuine $g$-dimensional abelian variety ($\Pic^0_{C/k}$), and no other route in this chapter produces it. In particular the critical path here is \emph{independent of the genus-$0$ route choice} --- the genus-$0$ arm is decoupled onto the trivial object $\Spec k$ (\cref{def:genusZeroWitness}) and the characteristic-free rigidity statement \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}, neither of which touches the Picard engine. Route~A is the single highest-cost build-out, since Hilbert/Quot schemes are upstream of much of modern algebraic geometry; but once present, the rest of Route A (identity component, smoothness, properness, universal property --- the last being the Albanese property of $\Pic^0$, Milne \emph{Abelian Varieties} Proposition~6.1) follows by classical arguments already widely formalised in adjacent settings.
434	    \item[\textbf{($\gamma$)}] \emph{The rigidity statement for morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$.} The genus-$0$ arm needs \emph{only} this rigidity \emph{statement}, \textbf{decoupled} from both the cotangent-vanishing pile and the Picard representability of ($\alpha$): its witness object is the trivial $\Spec k$, and the statement is the project's named, characteristic-free declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (\cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}), carrying $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and \emph{no} $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$ hypothesis (the older \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, still carrying both $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$, is the retained fallback-(a) artifact). The \textbf{committed} proof route is the characteristic-free Milne \S I.3 rigidity chain (cube-free) of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (route~(c): headline \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, Milne Proposition~3.10 specialised to $\mathbb P^1$), which uses neither the theorem of the cube, nor Serre duality, nor representability. The differential decomposition into the shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} --- cotangent triviality of a group scheme over $\bar k$ via the functorial shear iso; scheme-level $\mathrm d f = 0 \Rightarrow$ $f$ factors through $\Spec \bar k$ via the \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass; characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$ --- is the \textbf{fallback} route~(a), off the critical path but retained in the tree. The genus-$0$ arm of the witness existence is packaged as \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}), which over a general base field $k$ \emph{descends} the over-$\bar k$ rigidity conclusion to $k$: for each $k$-rational marked point $P \in C(k)$ it base-changes the data $(C, A, f, P)$ along $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (genus is stable under base change for a geometrically irreducible curve, so $C_{\bar k}$ is again a genus-$0$ curve), applies \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} over $\bar k$ to conclude $f_{\bar k}$ is constant, then descends the morphism equality back to $k$ along the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (Sub-step~C.2.f, whose closing move is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}). The $C(k) = \emptyset$ branch is vacuously true (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$). Independently of ($\alpha$), establishing ($\gamma$) together with the descent step (C.2.f --- a multi-iteration base-change sub-build, not a $\sim 2$-line step; see the re-costing there) lets the genus-$0$ part of the witness be discharged unconditionally via \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}; the higher-genus part is packaged as \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.positiveGenusWitness}, iter-134 scaffold) whose body closure is gated on Route A ($\alpha$). Per the iter-135 body restructure of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, the witness existence is now a case-split $\mathtt{by\_cases}\ h : \genus\,C = 0$ delegating to \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} and \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} respectively, converting the formerly inline \texttt{sorry} into honest delegation to the two named scaffolds.
435	    \item[\textbf{($\beta$, historical only --- not pursued).}] \emph{Symmetric powers of schemes plus finite-group-scheme quotients plus Stein factorisation for proper morphisms.} Documented as a historical alternative not pursued by the project (Route B above); see \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} Route B for context. This entry is retained for scholarly continuity only; under the iter-144 disposition, ($\beta$) is \emph{not} a build-out the project is gating any of its deliverables on, and ($\beta$) does not appear on any active critical path.
436	  \end{itemize}
437	
438	  In sum: Mathlib currently contains neither of the two active infrastructure builds (($\alpha$) and ($\gamma$)), and the existence statement is recorded here as the project's single explicit foundational hypothesis. It backs all downstream consequences --- the four protected instances on $\Jac(C)$ (Theorems~\ref{thm:Jacobian_grpObj}--\ref{thm:Jacobian_geomIrred}), and the Abel--Jacobi map and its universal property of Chapter~\ref{chap:AbelJacobi} --- and is discharged once ($\alpha$) and ($\gamma$) both land. # Blueprint Review Report

## Slug

## Top-level summaries

### Incomplete parts
- `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` / `lem:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne Thm 3.2, `\lean{rationalMap_to_av_extends}`): **NOT prover-ready — it is still an open research question, not an under-specified proof.** The stated proof = "Theorem 3.1 + Lemma 3.3". Lemma 3.3 (codim-1 indeterminacy of a rational map into a group variety) is proved in Milne via Weil-divisor theory (`div(f)₀ − div(f)₁`, prime divisors, tangent cones), for which `rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap` itself states Mathlib has **no usable API**. The remark's "pointwise valuative side-step" is explicitly posed as *"An open question for the prover"* — i.e. there is no decided formalisation path. No Lean decl exists. This block must **not** enter prover objectives until the pointwise-valuative approach is fleshed into a concrete sub-lemma stack (or the divisor gap is otherwise resolved).
- `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` / `lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial` (Milne Prop 3.9 core, `\lean{morphism_Ga_to_av_const}`): proof is **not detailed enough to formalise** (see "Proofs lacking detail"). No Lean decl exists.
- `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` / `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1`: genus-0 ⟹ ℙ¹ is a genuine Riemann–Roch sub-build with no Mathlib RR (the chapter's own `rmk:genusZero_iso_subbuild` says so). Body is `sorry`; not prover-ready as a short step.
- `Jacobian.tex` Route A (FGA engine, §"Route A" A.1–A.4 + `def:positiveGenusWitness`): the FGA representability engine (RelativeSpec / Quot / Hilbert / `Pic` representability / identity component `Pic⁰` / degree map / Albanese UP) exists only as **prose itemize sketches**, with zero prover-ready declaration blocks. Strategy labels this "blueprint sketch-level" and schedules expansion *after* the genus-0 stack; flagged here so the planner expands it before any positive-genus FGA prover lane (see Unstarted-phase proposal). Not blocking this iter.

### Proofs lacking detail
- `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` / `lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial` (proof, chapter lines ~858–886): the closing **𝔾_a/𝔾_m incompatibility ⟹ f constant** step is a single-sentence parenthetical ("an additive-group homomorphism … that is simultaneously … a multiplicative-group homomorphism … forces the image to be a single point"). It is NOT decomposed into a formalizable argument. Specifically missing:
  1. No explicit **`Hom(𝔾_a, A) = 0`** sub-lemma (a homomorphism from the additive group to an abelian variety is trivial) — the natural formal target the collision should reduce to.
  2. The 𝔾_m side ("the same additive-defect argument applied to the multiplicative structure gives `f(xy)=f(x)+f(y)+c`") is **asserted, not derived**: it silently needs a *second* application of `lem:rational_map_to_av_extends` (extending the multiplicative-defect map over ℙ¹×ℙ¹), which the prose glosses over.
  3. The **group-scheme infrastructure is assumed without provenance**: a concrete ℙ¹, 𝔾_a and 𝔾_m as group objects, the open immersions 𝔾_a ↪ ℙ¹ and 𝔾_m ↪ ℙ¹, and where the additive/multiplicative laws come from in Lean. The chapter gives no `\lean{...}` hint, no definition block, and no `% SOURCE` for any of these. STRATEGY records "the additive-group structure on 𝔸¹ and the triviality lemma are to build" — i.e. it is to-build infra with no blueprint coverage.
  A blueprint-writer must break this into sub-lemmas: (a) `Hom(𝔾_a, A) = 0`; (b) the 𝔾_a/𝔾_m group-object defs + open immersions into ℙ¹ (with `\lean` hints / sources or an explicit "Archon-original infra" tag); (c) the multiplicative-defect extension as its own step; (d) the concrete collision arithmetic that yields constancy.
- `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` / `lem:rational_map_to_av_extends` (proof): see Incomplete parts — the proof names the right ingredients but the load-bearing Lemma 3.3 step has no executable Mathlib path; this is research-level, not merely "vague".

## Unstarted-phase blueprint proposals

### Proposed chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/Pic_RelativeSpec.tex` (entry chapter of the Route A engine)

**Covers**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Pic/RelativeSpec.lean` (expected new file; entry point of the FGA engine — exact path TBD by planner)
**Strategy phase**: Route A — Picard/Quot/Hilbert FGA engine (positive-genus OBJECT)
**Why now**: Strategy explicitly schedules Route A expansion *after* the genus-0 stack closes, and the genus-0 frontier is itself currently blocked (this iter's two frontier blocks are not prover-ready). So **the planner may legitimately defer this proposal** with the one-line rationale "Route A expansion deferred per STRATEGY.md §M3 until genus-0 stack closes; FGA engine is sketch-level by design and feeds no active prover lane." I surface the seed now so it is ready the moment genus-0 lands; this is the single largest unwritten phase (~5100+ LOC) and writing its entry chapter early is the cheapest action once it becomes active.

**Key declarations** (in dependency order — speculative seed):
1. `\definition` `\label{def:relativeSpec}` — the relative-Spec functor `RelativeSpec : QCoh-Algebra → Sch/S`, A.1 prerequisite (~700–1100 LOC per iter-123 audit). `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.RelativeSpec}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure, FGA Explained §5 (`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.md`); Stacks `01LL`/`01LQ`.
2. `\definition` `\label{def:relativePicardFunctor}` — `Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) = Pic(C_T)/π*Pic(T)`. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.relativePicardFunctor}` [expected]. Source: Kleiman, FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2 (`references/kleiman-picard.md`).
3. `\theorem` `\label{thm:pic_representable}` — FGA representability of `Pic_{C/k}` by a `k`-group scheme l.f.t. (A.2). `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Pic.representable}` [expected]. Source: Kleiman §9.4 (`references/kleiman-picard.md`, p.262 of FGA-explained).
4. `\definition` `\label{def:pic0}` + `\theorem` `\label{thm:pic0_abelianVariety}` — identity component `Pic⁰`, degree map `Pic → ℤ`, smoothness + properness + dim = g (A.3). `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0}` [expected]. Source: Kleiman §9.5 (`references/kleiman-picard.md`); `lem:agps`/`prp:pic0` quotes already in `Jacobian.tex`.
5. `\theorem` `\label{thm:albanese_universal_property}` — the Albanese UP of `Pic⁰` (A.4), Milne Prop 6.1/6.4. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.isAlbanese}` [expected]. Source: Milne §III.6 (`references/abelian-varieties.md`). **No-regret note:** this UP reuses Route C's `lem:hom_additivity_over_product` (Cor 1.5) + `lem:av_regular_map_is_hom` (Cor 1.2) + `lem:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Thm 3.2) and is cube-free (per `rmk:cube_not_needed`).

**`\uses` skeleton**:

- `def:pic0` uses `thm:pic_representable`
- `thm:pic0_abelianVariety` uses `def:pic0`

**Main theorem proof strategy**: Build the Quot/Hilbert engine (Nitsure §4–5: generic flatness + flattening stratification, then Quot construction), use it to represent `Pic_{C/k}` (Grothendieck FGA-232), extract the identity component `Pic⁰` as the kernel of the locally-constant degree map, prove smoothness via the deformation-theoretic identification of the tangent space at the identity with `H¹(C,O_C)` (already built in the project's cohomology chapters) + homogeneity, then derive the Albanese UP from the proven Rigidity-Lemma chain (shared with Route C). This is the dominant positive-genus cost and the riskiest, least-Mathlib piece (A.2 representability).

- `references/kleiman-picard.md` → `.pdf`, §4 (existence) §5 (`Pic⁰`) — FGA `Pic` representability.

**Subphase choices exposed**:

Recommend splitting into (i) Quot/Hilbert engine, (ii) `Pic`/`Pic⁰` representability, (iii) Albanese UP — at least three chapters.
